title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo’s injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Triston Casas is recovering from an intercostal strain and will resume hitting from a tee on Monday.
Johan Oviedo will meet his Tommy John surgeon after experiencing a right elbow strain and decreased pitch velocity.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval are nearing rehab outings, with Sandoval starting in Double-A Portland this Sunday.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, a 22-year-old cancer survivor from Dedham, is running the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Diagnosed with aggressive thyroid cancer at 19, Taylor's successful treatment at MGH allowed her to graduate from Harvard.
Taylor's marathon participation aims to honor her doctors and the Adolescent and Young Adults (AYA) survivorship program.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie from South Boston is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team BMC to support Boston Medical Center.
Funds raised will aid BMC's safety-net mission and support social workers assisting patients in crisis.
Beattie aims to raise $10,000 through various events, emphasizing the essential role of social workers in healthcare.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium, continuing their search for a first NWSL win.
Coach Filipa Patão emphasized patience and strategic play in the attacking third after the team failed to register a shot on target.
Forward Amanda Gutierres struggled with passing accuracy, with highlights on her need to stay nearer to the goal area.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Suspended attorney Henry Rappa Jr. charged with embezzling hundreds of thousands from clients in Melrose, prosecutors state.
Rappa allegedly misused client funds despite court orders to cease practicing law and close trust accounts.
Rappa continued unauthorized legal practice, including a mishandled real estate closing, retaining $21,300 improperly.

title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eugene Mirman was rescued from a burning car after crashing into a Bedford, NH toll plaza.
Mirman thanked rescuers via social media, humorously commenting on his reduced screen time.
The crash is under investigation; no charges have been filed, police seek information from the public.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Jonathan G. Newcomb, 53, assaulted a New Hampshire State Trooper in Dover, resulting in multiple charges.
The attack occurred in a parking lot on Dover Point Road near Route 16, involving a stick and metal tool.
Newcomb faces felony and misdemeanor charges, including criminal threatening and attempting to take an officer's firearm.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, from Lynnfield is running the Boston Marathon to support Mass General's new program for cancer treatment.
Mallett runs in honor of Sharla Sieve, who died from Stage 4 metastatic colorectal cancer in December 2022.
Last year, Mallett completed the marathon despite an injury, and has run four total marathons since starting in 2023.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect Patriots to trade for Eagles' A.J. Brown after June 1 due to salary cap implications.
Patriots' interest in A.J. Brown linked to head coach Mike Vrabel's past connection, but the team remains discreet.
Eagles reportedly seek a first-round pick for A.J. Brown; Patriots may back out if asking price remains high.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Amanda Seyfried stars in 'The Testament of Ann Lee,' now streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,' directed by Nia DaCosta, is available on Netflix.
Ken Burns' documentary 'Henry David Thoreau' streams on PBS with narration by George Clooney.
